text:
Here's a fun little story.

Once upon a time in a small seaside town, there was a curious raccoon named Raj. Raj loved exploring piles of oldbooks and listening to tales of big cities. One summer afternoon, he stumbled upon an ancient, leather-bound cookbook from London. Intriced by an unusual recipe calling for "London fog pearls," Raj decided to try it.

He spent hours collecting the ethereal little pearls from mysterious nights. It wasn't easy. One moonlit hour at the old church graveyard yielded just a few. Raj excitedly returned home, crafting a ghostly white oulettes.

His human friend, an old biologist interested in myths, loved them. They became a favorite. But one foggy night, while listening to Raj tell stories, the biologist disappeared. Raj was scared. He searchied all night until dawn. Off passed a big bad owl. Into the fog he went. Finally, the light of dawn stole away the fog. There was the biologist,
